task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2231: feat: Document parsing layer for RAG — PPTX + PDF extraction (#487 sub-1) ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2231` 3. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2231 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 4. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 5. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: feat: Document parsing layer for RAG — PPTX + PDF extraction (#487 sub-1) (#2231)"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2231` 8. Open PR via GraphQL (same-org fork — `gh pr create` doesn't work): ```bash gh api graphql -f query=' mutation CreatePR { createPullRequest(input: { repositoryId: "R_kgDON3X_Ow" baseRefName: "dev" headRefName: "sophie/issue-2231" headRepositoryId: "R_kgDORH2m4g" title: "feat: feat: Document parsing layer for RAG — PPTX + PDF extraction (#487 sub-1)" body: "Closes #2231\n\nImplemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan." }) { pullRequest { url number } } }' ``` ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary Extend existing RAG infrastructure with a document parsing layer following established service patterns (factory + class). Use local parsers (officeparser, pdf.js-extract) as primary with quality-gated fallback to LlamaParse API. Preserve slide/page boundaries as semantic chunk units with rich metadata for filtering and context. Wire new `embedUploadedDocument()` function into existing embedding pipeline without modifying `embedDocument()` or `querySimilar()`.
Design Choices
Design Choice 1: Quality Gate Threshold **Options Considered:** 1. Hard-coded threshold (e.g., 50 chars/page) with no configuration 2. Configurable threshold via environment variable or constant 3. Adaptive threshold based on file size ratio **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** Use a configurable constant (`MIN_CHARS_PER_PAGE`) that can be easily adjusted during testing, defaulting to 50 chars/page as specified in the ticket.
Design Choice 2: LlamaParse API Error Handling **Options Considered:** 1. Throw error and fail the entire operation 2. Return partial results from local parsing with a warning 3. Retry with exponential backoff, then fail gracefully **Chosen Option:** 3 **Rationale:** Use the existing retry utility from `packages/mastra/src/resilience/retry.ts` with exponential backoff for transient errors, then throw for non-retryable errors (auth, validation). This aligns with established patterns.
💡 User Tips Regenerate the plan with different choices with `@coderabbitai `. ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Parser Foundation & Infrastructure Establish the type system, dependencies, and base service structure that all parsers will build upon. This phase creates the architectural skeleton without implementing parsing logic.
Task 1: Define Core Types and Interfaces Create TypeScript interfaces that define the contract for document parsing across all formats. - Create `packages/mastra/src/rag/parsers/types.ts` with `ParseResult`, `DocumentChunk`, and `DocumentMetadata` interfaces as specified in the ticket - Define `ParserStrategy` interface with async `parse(buffer: Buffer): Promise` method - Add Zod schemas for runtime validation of parsed results - Include `SupportedFormat` type union: `"pptx" | "pdf"`
Task 2: Install Dependencies Add required npm packages for document parsing to the Mastra package. - Add `officeparser` (v6.x) to `packages/mastra/package.json` for PPTX parsing - Add `pdf.js-extract` to `packages/mastra/package.json` for PDF parsing - Update `.env.example` at project root with `LLAMAPARSE_API_KEY` (optional) and add documentation comment
Task 3: Create Document Parser Service Shell Set up the main parser service following the factory function + class pattern established in the codebase. - Create `packages/mastra/src/rag/parsers/document-parser.ts` with `createDocumentParserService()` factory function - Implement `DocumentParserService` class with `parseDocument(buffer: Buffer, format: SupportedFormat, options?: ParseOptions)` method signature - Include structured logging with namespace `"document-parser"` following patterns from `packages/otp/src/server/otp.service.ts` - Add format detection and routing logic (switch on format to delegate to specific parsers)
Task 4: Set Up Barrel Exports Create module exports for the parsers directory to enable clean imports. - Create `packages/mastra/src/rag/parsers/index.ts` with exports for types, service factory, and parser utilities - Update `packages/mastra/src/rag.ts` to re-export parser functionality for deep imports
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` This phase establishes the architectural foundation for document parsing including types, dependencies, and service structure. **Type System Setup:** - In `packages/mastra/src/rag/parsers/types.ts`, define the core interfaces: - `ParseResult` interface with `chunks` array and `metadata` object - `DocumentChunk` interface for individual text chunks with metadata - `DocumentMetadata` interface for document-level information - `ParserStrategy` interface with async `parse(buffer: Buffer): Promise` method - `SupportedFormat` type union: `"pptx" | "pdf"` - Add Zod schemas for runtime validation of all interfaces **Dependencies:** - Add `officeparser` (v6.x) and `pdf.js-extract` to `packages/mastra/package.json` - Update `.env.example` with `LLAMAPARSE_API_KEY` (optional) with documentation comment **Service Structure:** - In `packages/mastra/src/rag/parsers/document-parser.ts`, create: - `createDocumentParserService()` factory function following codebase patterns - `DocumentParserService` class with `parseDocument(buffer: Buffer, format: SupportedFormat, options?: ParseOptions)` method signature - Structured logging with namespace `"document-parser"` following patterns from `packages/otp/src/server/otp.service.ts` - Format detection and routing logic using switch statement to delegate to specific parsers **Module Exports:** - Create `packages/mastra/src/rag/parsers/index.ts` with barrel exports for types, service factory, and utilities - Update `packages/mastra/src/rag.ts` to re-export parser functionality ```
### Phase 2: Format-Specific Parsers with Quality Gate Implement the actual parsing logic for each document format, plus the LlamaParse fallback with automatic quality-based escalation.
